source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 3.6 Check your Knowledge (pp. 26-27)

questions.q: An operating system is a set of programs for managing the available resources of a computer.
The operating system is necessary to... which pair is actually on the OS's job list?
options: Manage physical and virtual memory, and collect information about the programs used or in use
Allow direct access to peripherals, and manage memory
Subcontract the management of tasks to the processor, and collect information
Allow direct access to peripherals, and subcontract task management to the processor
questions.why: Two traps from the guide's own quiz: the OS INTERCEPTS access to peripherals (software rarely touches hardware directly), and the OS itself schedules processes - it does not subcontract task management to the processor.

questions.q: Among these personalities, which ones participated in the development of UNIX?
options: Andrew Stuart Tanenbaum and Brian Kernighan
questions.why: Thompson and Ritchie began UNIX; Kernighan joined later. Torvalds created Linux, Tanenbaum MINIX. Lionel Richie sang 'Hello' - different Richie.

questions.q: The original nationality of Linus Torvalds, creator of the Linux kernel, is:
questions.why: A Finnish student, per the 1991 history bullet.

questions.q: Which of the following distributions is the oldest?
questions.why: The chapter only dated Debian (1993), but Slackware shipped in July 1993, weeks before Debian was announced - the one option the chapter never taught. Red Hat 1994, Arch 2002.

questions.q: Is the Linux kernel multi-tasking, multi-user, multi-processor, multi-core, cross-platform, open?
questions.why: Section 3.2.3: time-sharing multi-task, multi-user (from MULTICS), multi-processor or multi-core, multi-platform via C, and open (POSIX, TCP/IP, NFS, Samba).
...but the opposite is not true: open source means the source is available under conditions, distinct from the GPL's freedoms.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a shell?
questions.why: The guide's own joke: Jason Bourne is a film franchise; Steve Bourne wrote the Bourne shell. bash, csh and ksh are real.
